hook: Resale landed is still moving — but the gap to peak is widening

slides.sub: Faber Hills, Sentosa Cove, D5 terraces — the data shows where sellers are blinking first.

slides.lead: Resale landed transactions are clearing — but at noticeably softer psf.

slides.post: vs S$17.4M peak print in Feb 2023 — same street, same 99-yr tenure, Coral Island
slides.source: URA caveats · Coral Island · POV Guy analysis

slides.title: Three signals in this week's data

items.body: Sentosa Cove is off ~15% from its 2022-23 peak: Feb 2025 at S$14.8M vs S$17.4M (Feb 2023), same size band.
Faber Hills semi-ds transacted at S$6.45M–S$7.1M in H2 2025, below the S$7.8M–S$8.4M cluster in late 2024.
D5 freehold terraces (Springwood, Jalan Mat Jambol) are still clearing — Mar 2026 at S$5.58M — proving OCR demand holds.

slides.title: Top resale landed closes — last 6 months

slides.label: The real story
slides.punch: Faber Hills and Sentosa Cove are both clearing stock, but buyers are dictating the close. Gaps of 10–15% below 2023 highs are now the norm, not the exception.
slides.punch_bold: In this market, the first ask is just a starting point.

items.body: Faber Hills logged 6 semi-d caveats in H2 2024 alone — volume is healthy even as psf softens.
D5 freehold terraces (Springwood) posted 4 caveats from mid-2025 to Mar 2026 — consistent, not distressed.
Sentosa Cove D4 sees thin volume — 1 caveat in 2025 YTD — so each print moves the needle significantly.

slides.pre: Watching landed in D4 / D5 / D10?

slides.sub: If you know anyone weighing a landed purchase in D4 or D5 this month, send them this.

caption:
📉 Resale landed is still clearing — but buyers are setting the price now, not sellers.

• Sentosa Cove (Feb 2025): S$14.8M — down ~15% from the S$17.4M peak in Feb 2023
• Faber Hills semi-ds: S$6.45M–S$7.1M in H2 2025, vs S$7.8M–S$8.4M in late 2024
• D5 freehold terraces still transacting — Springwood hit S$5.58M in Mar 2026

Volume is there. But the days of sellers anchoring to 2023 highs are over — buyers know it, and the caveats prove it.
